summary: Large fighter-system cleanup focused on replacing anonymous fighter/player symbols and struct fields with descriptive names while keeping assembly callsites and headers synchronized. The PR renamed model-scale helpers, fighter data globals, costume-list data, Player_LoadPlayerCoords, several early Fighter_* routines, and many Fighter/ftCommonData fields, plus cleaned local variable names and stale comments in fighter.c. There were no review comments in the slice, so motivation beyond the title and diff is inferred from cleanup/renaming evidence.
what_changed: Renamed func_8007F694 to Fighter_GetModelScale and updated C and assembly callsites.
Renamed func_80067BB4 to Fighter_UpdateModelScale and updated many fighter/chara assembly and C callsites.
Renamed lbl_804598B8 to gFtDataList and propagated @ha/@l references across asm files such as ftkirby.s, ftyoshi.s, ftdata.s, and code_8008521C.s.
Renamed lbl_803C0EC0 to CostumeListsForeachCharacter and updated data declarations plus costume-list users in ftlib.c and assembly.
Renamed Player_800326CC to Player_LoadPlayerCoords in player.c/player.h and updated references in ground.c and assembly.

Renamed early fighter routines in fighter.h, including Fighter_FirstInitialize_80067A84, Fighter_LoadCommonData, Fighter_ResetInputData_80068854, Fighter_UnkProcessDeath_80068354, Fighter_UnkUpdateCostumeJoint_800686E4, Fighter_UnkUpdateVecFromBones_8006876C, and Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914.

Improved Fighter and ftCommonData field names: x260_startShieldHealth, x6DC_colorsByPlayer, x14_action_id, x1C_ftStateList, x20_ftStateList, x108_costume_joint, x610_color_rgba, stick/substick coordinate fields, and x672_input_timer_counter.
Renamed helper structs from vague temporary names toward domain terms, including UnkCostumeStruct, UnkCostumeList, and FtState.

Cleaned fighter.c local variables from register-derived names such as fighter_r31/temp_f1/vec_sp20 into semantic names such as fighter1/fighter2/model_size/vec, and removed many address-style comment markers.
Removed commented-out debug print/blank lines from tools/frank.py without changing its visible algorithm in the diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Propagated each symbol rename through C headers, C implementation files, and raw assembly callsites in the same PR, avoiding split-brain naming where C and asm disagree.

Used descriptive names while retaining addresses in some partially understood Fighter_* functions, e.g. Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914, preserving searchability and uncertainty instead of overclaiming behavior.
Renamed globals at their actual data definitions and all @ha/@l assembly relocations, which is the safe pattern for decomp projects where labels are referenced directly from asm.

Converted repeated magic/anonymous struct fields into narrowly scoped semantic names only where usage supported it, such as model scale, shield health, costume joints, costume lists, input stick coordinates, and player colors.
Kept functional call targets unchanged at the machine-code level while improving labels, making the cleanup valuable for future matching without implying a behavioral rewrite.
decomp_lessons: When a function name becomes known, update every caller in asm, headers, and C immediately; stale func_######## names make later matching and review much harder.
If semantics are plausible but not fully proven, combine a subsystem prefix and original address, as in Fighter_UnkProcessDeath_80068354, rather than inventing a fully confident name.
Global data names should describe the table role, not just the address: gFtDataList and CostumeListsForeachCharacter are much more useful than lbl_804598B8 and lbl_803C0EC0.

Struct field naming can be incremental: replacing x260 with x260_startShieldHealth or x108_costume_archive with x108_costume_joint documents discovered meaning without requiring the full struct to be solved.
Cleanup-only PRs can still be high leverage if they reduce register-derived locals, address comments, and anonymous temporaries in large core files like fighter.c.
Renaming a player helper such as Player_LoadPlayerCoords should include downstream stage/ground and debug asm references, not only player.c/player.h.
naming_conventions: Fighter subsystem functions use the Fighter_ prefix, often with the original address suffix for partially understood routines.
Known player routines use Player_ plus an action phrase, e.g. Player_LoadPlayerCoords.
Known globals may use a g prefix, e.g. gFtDataList.
Data tables can use descriptive PascalCase-style names, e.g. CostumeListsForeachCharacter.
Partially understood types can retain Unk but should include the known domain, e.g. UnkCostumeStruct and UnkCostumeList.
Struct fields keep offset prefixes while adding meaning, e.g. x260_startShieldHealth, x1C_ftStateList, x620_lstick_x, x638_lsubStick_x.
Boolean/flag and ID fields should encode role where evident, e.g. action_id, move_id, flags, x4_flags, x9_flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly-only users were updated by changing symbol names on existing branch/call and relocation comments without altering instruction bytes.
For globals referenced by lis/addi pairs, both @ha and @l operands were renamed together from lbl_804598B8 to gFtDataList or lbl_803C0EC0 to CostumeListsForeachCharacter.
Callsite updates preserved original branch offsets while replacing labels, e.g. bl func_80067BB4 became bl Fighter_UpdateModelScale and bl func_8007F694 became bl Fighter_GetModelScale.
Data symbol declarations in asm were renamed at the source label, not just at use sites, keeping global declarations and references consistent.
The cleanup of fighter.c local variables suggests using semantic locals to make decompiled control/data flow readable while retaining matching behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully rename Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914 or related Fighter_Unk* routines.
Search for later struct work around FtState, UnkCostumeList, and CostumeListsForeachCharacter.
Check whether x27C in ftCommonData was later named alongside x260_startShieldHealth.
Search for remaining lbl_804598B8, lbl_803C0EC0, func_80067BB4, func_8007F694, and Player_800326CC references after this PR.
Look for follow-up Fighter.c cleanup commits by snuffysasa.
